### Post by blkno1 on 2007-06-12
OK got my new Dell E1505N with a Nvidia Go 7300, did a fresh install of Feisty  I have Beryl install but I don't get and window borders on any of the open windows.

I tried adding Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True" to the Screen section as I saw on another post that could be the problem, however didnt help me  :(

Any ideas?

my xorg.conf

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Feb 26 23:38:46 PST 2007

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"

    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"

    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver         "synaptics"
    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
    Option         "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY

Section "Monitor"

    HorizSync       28.0 - 84.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "nVidia Corporation G72M [Quadro NVS 110M/GeForce Go 7300]"

EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
```

### Post by jarocooke on 2007-06-12
Two things you could try.

1. Under the sessions menu option (in administration I think), add BOTH beryl and beryl-manager.

2. Make sure that you have installed the emerald package (default used by beryl).

BTW, I have had a similar problem when I first installed beryl, I am pretty sure it was number 2 above that was causing the problem, but I can't remember.:-k

---

### Post by camarojones on 2007-06-12
With just the command Beryl, mine does the same thing. I changed it to beryl-manager, and it works like a charm.

---
